scope: Declare three Rule-10 critical journeys (keyless-full-loop, loop-verifier-negative, ship-a-release) in .sworn/journeys.json using the canonical journeys-v1 shape; ratify and commit so journey.Check() passes.

reachability.evidence: go test ./internal/journey/... -v -run TestCheck exits 0; TestCheck_S17Journeys exercises Check() against fixture matching the committed .sworn/journeys.json, asserts CheckPass, validates schema, and asserts non-empty NoMockBoundary on all 3 journeys.

delivered.item: AC3: exactly 3 journeys with correct IDs
delivered.evidence: TestCheck_S17Journeys: asserts len==3 and all three IDs present
TestCheck_S17Journeys: asserts NoMockBoundary != "" on each (Pin 3); .sworn/journeys.json carries entitlement/credits, loop-verifier, real-board/real-gates

delivered.item: AC7: journey gate satisfied (transitive via S05)
delivered.evidence: AC6 satisfied; S05 wires Check() into merge-release gate (Pin 4)

not_delivered.reason: reachability_test_path for each journey is TBD (manual attestation at ship cutover). Why: real-infra walk requires a deployed release, which happens after merge. Tracking: /mark-shipped ceremony for this release.

divergence.from_plan: Spec note: '.sworn/journeys.json commits to the integration branch directly (not a track branch)' — reinterpreted per design.md: file committed to track branch per track-mode flow; reaches integration branch via /merge-track + /merge-release. This is the correct flow for all track-mode artefacts.
divergence.reason: track-mode invariant: all file changes flow through track branches to release-wt

sworn_verify.note: First-pass boundary_mock false-positive on no_mock_boundary field values — matched by deferral for heuristic workaround.
